corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

For future work, we are curious about whether we could extend VisualBERT to image-only tasks, such as scene graph parsing and situation recognition.
In this section we introduce VisualBERT, a model for learning joint contextualized representations of vision and language.
For the image representations, each dataset we study has a different standard object detector to generate region proposals and region features.

For each dataset, we evaluate three variants of our model: VisualBERT: The full model with parameter initialization from BERT that undergoes pre-training on COCO, pre-training on the task data, and fine-tuning for the task.
Then we use Flickr30K as a diagnostic dataset to understand whether VisualBERT's pre-training phase actually allows the model to learn implicit alignments between bounding regions and text phrases.
To generate these examples, Figure : Accuracy of attention heads of VisualBERT for predicting four specific dependency relationships ("pobj", "amod", "nsubj", and "dobj") across modality.
We show the best heads of 6 layers that achieve the highest entity grounding accuracy.
We propose VisualBERT, a simple and flexible framework for modeling a broad range of vision-and-language tasks.
We propose VisualBERT, a simple and flexible framework for modeling a broad range of vision-and-language tasks.
We further propose two visually-grounded language model objectives for pre-training VisualBERT on image caption data.
Experiments on four vision-and-language tasks including VQA, VCR, NLVR 2 , and Flickr30K show that VisualBERT outperforms or rivals with state-of-the-art models while being significantly simpler.
Experiments on four vision-and-language tasks including VQA, VCR, NLVR 2 , and Flickr30K show that VisualBERT outperforms or rivals with state-of-the-art models while being significantly simpler.

Beyond simply recognizing what objects are present , vision-and-language tasks, such as captioning , visual question answering , and visual reasoning , challenge systems to understand a wide range of detailed semantics of an image, including objects, attributes, parts, spatial relationships, actions and intentions, and how all of these concepts are referred to and grounded in natural language.

Beyond simply recognizing what objects are present , vision-and-language tasks, such as captioning , visual question answering , and visual reasoning , challenge systems to understand a wide range of detailed semantics of an image, including objects, attributes, parts, spatial relationships, actions and intentions, and how all of these concepts are referred to and grounded in natural language.
In this paper, we propose VisualBERT, a simple and flexible model designed for capturing rich semantics in the image and associated text.

We propose two visually-grounded language model objectives for pre-training: (1) part of the text is masked and the model learns to predict the masked words based on the remaining text and visual context; (2) the model is trained to determine whether the provided text matches the image.
Results demonstrate that by pre-training VisualBERT on the COCO image caption dataset , VisualBERT outperforms or rivals with the state-of-the-art models.
Results demonstrate that by pre-training VisualBERT on the COCO image caption dataset , VisualBERT outperforms or rivals with the state-of-the-art models.
Results demonstrate that by pre-training VisualBERT on the COCO image caption dataset , VisualBERT outperforms or rivals with the state-of-the-art models.
However, VideoBERT is evaluated on captioning for cooking videos, while we conduct comprehensive analysis on a variety of vision-and-language tasks.

Concurrently with our work, ViLBERT proposes to learn joint representation of images and text using a BERT-like architecture but has separate Transformers for vision and language that can only attend to each-other (resulting in twice the parameters).

They use a slightly different pre-training process Position Segment Token/Image A person hits a ball with a tennis racket Transformer … [CLS] a [MASK] [SEP] Objective 2 Objective 1 e1 + e2 + eN-1 + eN + … f1 + f2 + fK-1 + fK + … f'1 f'2 f'K-1 f'K … e'1 e'2 e'N-1 e'N Figure : The architecture of VisualBERT.

downstream_contract.intended_use: Feed this JSON to a later local open-source model to write an overview.
downstream_contract.grounding_rule: The downstream generator should write from evidence_units.location.sentence and context_window, not from unsupported outside knowledge.
downstream_contract.score_warning: Scores are ranking and filtering signals, not calibrated probabilities.
